commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer/security review of the permission-boundary shape; automation cannot supply the contributor's deployment proof.; Needs attention: The patch relaxes chmod failure handling around exec approvals and persistent state hardening, and the current helper does not verify that tolerated paths remain private enough.; Review finding: [P1] Validate permissions before swallowi... (sha=93dccf67bba3c9e3c0012ea669253e889318bc17)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `tryChmodSync` and rewires exec approvals plus task, flow, and plugin-state SQLite permission hardening to tolerate EPERM/EACCES/EROFS when access checks pass, with unit tests for the helper.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and the linked issue logs: current main rethrows non-Windows chmod EPERM in `ensureDir`, and the repo Kubernetes manifest uses the fsGroup/non-root PVC pattern. I did not run a live PVC reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer/security review of the permission-boundary shape; automation cannot supply the contributor's deployment proof.
